tasks.title: Restore mobile conversation state after reconnect
tasks.prompt:
You are working on the SyntaxSenpai project (a cross-platform AI companion app
built with Electron + React).

Your task: restore mobile conversation state after reconnecting or pairing again with the desktop app.

This behavior is currently incomplete. Do not stop at analysis. You must write code to disk.

Goal:
When a mobile device reconnects or pairs again with the desktop app, the mobile chat screen should resume the correct existing conversation for the active waifu/session instead of creating or showing an empty in-memory session. After hydration, the next mobile message should continue that same conversation cleanly.

Requirements:
1. Implement the missing end-to-end behavior so reconnecting mobile clients recover the correct existing conversation state for the active waifu/session.
2. The solution must use a real sync path between desktop and mobile, with the desktop remaining the source of truth for persisted conversation history.
3. Reuse existing persisted desktop conversation data when possible instead of inventing a parallel storage system.
4. Make the reconnect behavior deterministic when multiple previously persisted conversations or session candidates exist; do not pick an arbitrary conversation.
5. Reconcile restored state in a way that avoids obviously broken behavior such as duplicate history, switching to the wrong conversation, or sending the next message into a fresh local-only session after hydration.
6. Add or update a focused validation artifact such as a test or equivalent verification path.
7. Write the complete implementation to disk in the appropriate files.

Completion criteria:
- there is a real protocol or message-handling path that sends existing conversation state from desktop to mobile
- the mobile side consumes that state and hydrates the chat screen/history
- reconnecting does not fall back to an empty conversation when relevant persisted state exists
- post-hydration mobile sends continue the restored conversation rather than a new local-only one
- there is at least one desktop-side and one mobile-side code edit
- there is at least one added or updated validation artifact

tasks.title: End-to-end backup import and restore workflow
tasks.prompt:
You are working on the SyntaxSenpai project (a cross-platform AI companion app
built with Electron + React).

Your task: add an end-to-end desktop backup import and restore workflow.

This feature is currently incomplete. Do not stop at analysis. You must write code to disk.

Goal:
A desktop user should be able to export their persisted app data to JSON and later import that data back into the app through a real restore flow. The restore path should recover the relevant persisted state cleanly instead of only reading a file or writing raw JSON without integrating it into the existing runtime/storage flow.

Requirements:
1. Implement a real end-to-end import/restore workflow for persisted app data, not just file selection or generic JSON save/load helpers.
2. Reuse the existing backup/export/restore infrastructure and persisted data formats where possible instead of inventing a parallel format or storage path.
3. The restore behavior should handle both chat history and any other persisted state that is already part of the backup model.
4. Make the workflow reasonably safe: avoid obviously destructive behavior such as restoring arbitrary malformed payloads without validation or overwriting current state without a recovery path.
5. Ensure the restored data is actually visible to the app through the existing persistence/runtime path after import, rather than only being copied somewhere unused.
6. Add or update a focused validation artifact such as a test or equivalent verification path.
7. Write the complete implementation to disk in the appropriate files.

Completion criteria:
- there is a real desktop-triggerable import/restore path, not only low-level JSON dialog helpers
- existing backup or persisted-data infrastructure is reused rather than bypassed
- restored chat and related persisted state are written back through the app's real storage/runtime path
- malformed or clearly invalid restore inputs are not treated as successful restores
- there is at least one desktop-side and one runtime-side or storage-side code edit
- there is at least one added or updated validation artifact

tasks.title: End-to-end Azure OpenAI provider support
tasks.prompt:
You are working on the SyntaxSenpai project (a cross-platform AI companion app
built with Electron + React).

Your task: add end-to-end Azure OpenAI provider support.

This feature is currently incomplete. Do not stop at analysis. You must write code to disk.

Requirements:
1. Implement the missing Azure OpenAI provider behavior so it no longer throws "not yet fully implemented".
2. Make Azure OpenAI work end-to-end with the existing provider management flow.
3. Reuse existing OpenAI-compatible patterns where appropriate, but preserve Azure-specific behavior.
4. Add or update a focused validation path such as a test or equivalent code-level verification.
5. Write the complete implementation to disk in the appropriate files.

Completion criteria:
- there is a real Azure OpenAI provider implementation in ai-core
- there is at least one non-provider-file edit supporting end-to-end integration
- there is at least one added or updated validation artifact
